sequence.title: sequence
sequence.description:
sequence represents the order of events. It's always counting

 on read: the sequence of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
change_date is the timestamp when the object was changed

 on read: the timestamp of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

resourceOwner.title: resource_owner
resourceOwner.description: resource_owner is the organization or instance_id an object belongs to

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: creation_date is the timestamp where the first operation on the object was made

givenName.title: given_name
givenName.description: The given name is the first name of the user

familyName.title: family_name
familyName.description: The family name is the last name of the user

state.title: state
state.description: State of the user, for example active, inactive, locked, deleted, initial.

username.title: username
username.description: Username of the user, which can be globally unique or unique on organization level.

loginNames.title: login_names
loginNames.description: Possible usable login names for the user.

preferredLoginName.title: preferred_login_name
preferredLoginName.description: Preferred login name of the user.

email.title: email
email.description: Email of the user, if defined.

phone.title: phone
phone.description: Phone of the user, if defined.

passwordChangeRequired.title: password_change_required
passwordChangeRequired.description: User is required to change the used password on the next login.

passwordChanged.title: password_changed
passwordChanged.description: The time the user last changed their password.

mfaInitSkipped.title: mfa_init_skipped
mfaInitSkipped.description: The time the user last skipped MFA initialization.

urlTemplate.title: url_template
urlTemplate.description:
Optionally set a url_template, which will be used in the invite mail sent by Zitadel to guide the user to your invitation page.
 If no template is set and no previous code was created, the default Zitadel url will be used.

 The following placeholders can be used: UserID, OrgID, Code

applicationName.title: application_name
applicationName.description:
Optionally set an application name, which will be used in the invite mail sent by ZITADEL.
 If no application name is set and no previous code was created, Zitadel will be used as default.

givenName.title: given_name
givenName.description: The given name is the first name of the user

familyName.title: family_name
familyName.description: The family name is the last name of the user
